summary: Unmerged/superseded attempt to decompile fighter collision-related function func_8007B760 in src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c and add its prototype to ftcoll.h. The function retrieves a Fighter from a fighter GObj, conditionally raises fp->x1990 based on arg1, sets fp->x198C to 2, and calls func_800BFFD0(fp, 9, 0). A human comment states the PR was superseded by #576, so this record should be treated as historical context rather than accepted final code.
what_changed: Added a C implementation of func_8007B760 to src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c.
Added a prototype for func_8007B760 to src/melee/ft/ftcoll.h.
Implementation used getFighter(fighter_gobj) to obtain Fighter* fp.
Implementation compared arg1 against fp->x1990 and assigned fp->x1990 = arg1 only when arg1 was greater.
Implementation set fp->x198C = 2 and then called func_800BFFD0(fp, 9, 0).
The PR was closed without merge and explicitly described in a comment as superseded by #576.
smart_moves: Kept the implementation small and localized to ftcoll.c with the matching header declaration in ftcoll.h.
Preserved a decomp.me scratch link in a source comment, giving reviewers and future workers a way to inspect the matching attempt.
Used a conditional max-style update for fp->x1990 instead of overwriting unconditionally, matching the apparent semantics shown in the diff.
decomp_lessons: For small fighter utility functions, pair the .c implementation with an immediate header prototype update so later callsites can reference the symbol cleanly.
A simple scratch-derived implementation may still be superseded by another PR; check related PRs before reusing unmerged code.
When a PR is closed as superseded, treat its field names and signatures as tentative unless the superseding PR confirms them.

This function suggests fp->x198C and fp->x1990 are state/counter-like Fighter fields involved in the same behavior path as func_800BFFD0, but the exact semantic names are not established in this slice.
naming_conventions: Function remained named by address: func_8007B760.
Header declaration used the same raw symbol name as the implementation.
Arguments were named generically as fighter_gobj and arg1; no stronger semantic name was evidenced.
Existing file style around this area used raw field names such as x198C, x1990, and x182c_behavior where semantics were still unknown.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used getFighter(fighter_gobj) before accessing Fighter fields, consistent with fighter GObj helper patterns.
Used an explicit cast in the comparison, arg1 > (s32) fp->x1990, likely to match signed comparison behavior.
Implemented a max/update pattern for fp->x1990 followed by state assignment and a helper call.
Included the decomp.me scratch URL https://decomp.me/scratch/mL8R8 as matching evidence, but the slice does not include assembly details or reviewer confirmation.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.impact: The PR was closed without merge; future work should consult PR #576 for the accepted or newer version.

follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #576 for the superseding implementation of func_8007B760.
Search later history for func_8007B760 to see whether the signature stayed void func_8007B760(void* fighter_gobj, s32 arg1) or was refined to HSD_GObj*.
Search for uses of fp->x198C, fp->x1990, and func_800BFFD0(fp, 9, 0) to infer better field and function names.
Check whether the accepted version retained the signed cast on fp->x1990 comparison.
